system_instruction: Focus on psoriasis diagnosis, severity assessment, and treatment selection including topical and systemic therapies.

patient_persona.name: James Henderson
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Red, scaly patches on elbows, knees, and scalp for 5 years with recent worsening
patient_persona.background_story: Gradually worsening psoriasis, affecting his appearance and confidence in sales meetings. Multiple topical treatments have failed

initial_prompt: These red patches on my skin keep getting worse. They're all over my elbows, knees, and scalp. The creams my regular doctor gave me aren't helping anymore. It's really affecting my confidence at work.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Moderate to Severe Chronic Plaque Psoriasis

history_of_presenting_illness.location: Elbows, knees, scalp, lower back, nails
history_of_presenting_illness.character: Well-demarcated erythematous plaques with silvery scale, pruritic
history_of_presenting_illness.timing_and_duration: Chronic persistent with gradual worsening over 5 years
associated_symptoms: Joint pain and stiffness in fingers (suspected PsA)

medications: Betamethasone valerate 0.1% cream - previously effective, now minimal response

social_history.alcohol_use: 3-4 drinks weekly, especially for work events

physical_exam_findings.skin: Multiple erythematous plaques with silvery scale on extensor surfaces (elbows, knees), sacrum, scalp. Plaques vary from 2-8 cm in diameter. Auspitz sign present
physical_exam_findings.nail: Nail pitting on fingernails, onycholysis of right thumb, oil drop spots
physical_exam_findings.scalp: Thick plaques with scale extending 2cm beyond hairline
physical_exam_findings.joints: No synovitis or joint swelling, some stiffness in DIP joints of fingers

imaging_findings.hand_xray: No erosive changes or joint space narrowing

previous_treatments: UVB phototherapy - tried but inconvenient due to work schedule

end_session.patient_response: I'd like to try the systemic treatment. I'm tired of dealing with this.

critical_issue.response: Patient reports fever and pustules on skin. Possible pustular psoriasis or infection. Urgent evaluation required.

evaluation_criteria.Disease_Assessment: Accurate assessment of psoriasis severity using BSA, PASI, and DLQI scores
evaluation_criteria.Treatment_Escalation: Appropriate step-up from topical to systemic therapy based on severity
evaluation_criteria.Biologic_Therapy_Knowledge: Knowledge of biologic therapy options, mechanisms, and safety monitoring
evaluation_criteria.Comorbidity_Screening: Screening for psoriatic arthritis and associated comorbidities
evaluation_criteria.Patient_Counseling: Effective counseling on treatment expectations, side effects, and monitoring requirements
evaluation_criteria.Lifestyle_Modification: Discussion of trigger management and lifestyle factors affecting psoriasis

description: Acute allergic contact dermatitis assessment and management
system_instruction: Focus on identification of contact allergen, acute management of dermatitis, and prevention strategies.

patient_persona.name: Laura Bennett
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Itchy, blistering rash on arms and legs for 3 days
patient_persona.background_story: Was gardening in a new area of her yard 5 days ago. Noticed itchy red bumps 2 days later which have worsened into blisters

initial_prompt: I was gardening five days ago and now I have this horrible itchy rash with blisters on my arms and legs. It's spreading and I can't sleep because of the itching.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Acute Allergic Contact Dermatitis due to Poison Ivy (Urushiol)

history_of_presenting_illness.onset: 2 days after known gardening exposure, progressive over 3 days
history_of_presenting_illness.location: Linear streaks on forearms, lower legs, patch on ankle where socks meet skin
history_of_presenting_illness.character: Erythematous papules and vesicles, bullae in areas of heaviest exposure, intense pruritus

past_medical_history: Previous mild poison ivy 10 years ago

social_history.living_situation: Lives with husband, has large garden

physical_exam_findings.skin: Linear erythematous papules and vesicles on bilateral forearms and lower legs. Several 1-2 cm bullae on left ankle. Streaks of erythema where plant contact occurred. Mild edema of affected areas. No secondary infection
physical_exam_findings.distribution: Linear pattern consistent with plant brushing against skin. Interdigital spaces of hands also affected

lab_results.No_labs_indicated: Diagnosis is clinical based on history and presentation

treatment_plan.topical: High-potency corticosteroid (clobetasol) BID to affected areas for 7 days
treatment_plan.systemic: Prednisone 40mg daily x 5 days, then taper 20mg x 5 days, then 10mg x 5 days
treatment_plan.prevention: Education on plant identification, barrier creams, protective clothing, and washing exposed areas immediately

end_session.patient_response: Thank you! I'll stay away from the poison ivy and use the creams as prescribed.

critical_issue.response: Extensive facial involvement or systemic symptoms detected. Consider higher level of care.

evaluation_criteria.History_Taking: Thorough exposure history to identify potential allergens
evaluation_criteria.Diagnostic_Accuracy: Accurate recognition of allergic contact dermatitis vs irritant contact dermatitis vs other dermatoses
evaluation_criteria.Severity_Assessment: Appropriate assessment of extent and severity to determine treatment intensity
evaluation_criteria.Treatment_Selection: Appropriate selection of topical and systemic therapy based on severity
evaluation_criteria.Patient_Education: Effective counseling on allergen identification, avoidance strategies, and proper use of protective measures
evaluation_criteria.Prevention_Strategies: Comprehensive prevention plan including barrier creams, protective clothing, and post-exposure washing
